## COMP110 

This site will contain work and projects from my *COMP 110* course. 

## Summary of analysis

Students who report higher course difficulty and lower understanding tend to show stronger support for pre-lecture videos, suggesting these resources may help those who struggle most. Additionally, students who perceive the course pace as fast are also more likely to favor pre-lecture videos, indicating a need for flexible preparation tools. Overall, the data suggests a positive relationship between academic challenges and demand for supplementary learning materials. While the findings support implementing pre-lecture videos, they are correlational and would benefit from further testing to confirm their impact on student performance.

## Conclusion

From these visualizations, it appears that students who report higher difficulty and lower understanding tend to show stronger support for pre lecture videos. This suggests that students who struggle more in the course are more likely to value additional preparation resources. Additionally, students who feel the course is moving quickly also tend to show higher interest in pre lecture videos, indicating that these resources could help manage the pace of the course. Overall, the data suggests that pre lecture videos could provide meaningful support to students who need extra time or preparation before lectures. 

This analysis suggests that implementing pre lecture videos could create value for students in COMP110, especially those who find the course difficult or feel less confident in their understanding. The data moderately supports this idea, as students who report higher difficulty and lower understanding are more likely to believe pre lecture vidoes would be helpful. However, there are trade offs to consider. Creating these videos would require additional effort from instructors, and not all students may choose to use them. Additionally, this analysis shows relationships between variables but does not prove causation. In the future, this idea could be tested by implementing pre lecture videos and collecting new data to measure their actual impact on student learning and performance.
